display_label: Email Address
notes: Used as login credential; must be unique. Not on original paper form — added for system login.

display_label: Password
notes: Not a form field. Stored as bcrypt hash server-side. Never exposed to user. Added for authentication system.

display_label: I am a
notes: First screen on registration. Determines which form sections are shown. Not on original paper form.

display_label: Student Number / University ID
notes: Required for continuing, transferee, and returnee students. Optional for new students — auto-generate after enrollment. Used for CHED de-duplication and Registrar record matching. Regex ^\d{4}-\d{3,5}$ accommodates 3–5 digits after the dash; confirm exact format with Registrar's Office before deployment and update regex accordingly (Fix 2).

display_label: I consent to the OSWD processing my personal data under the Data Privacy Act of 2012.
notes: Required before any data is saved. Not on original paper form — added for RA 10173 (Data Privacy Act of 2012) compliance.

display_label: Consent Timestamp
notes: System-generated UTC datetime recorded at the exact moment student ticks consent_data_privacy. Never editable by user or admin. Required for RA 10173 (Data Privacy Act) audit trail. Data type corrected to datetime (was date — Fix 1).

display_label: Date
notes: Auto-filled with current system date. Shown read-only to user.

display_label: Birthdate
notes: Original form asks for Age only. Replaced with Birthdate (date picker) so age can be computed dynamically. Age is a derived/calculated field — do not store separately.

display_label: Age
notes: DERIVED FIELD — computed from birthdate at display time. Do not store in database. Original form field replaced by birthdate. Shown read-only.

display_label: Active Contact Number
notes: Philippine mobile format (09XXXXXXXXX). Separate from emergency contact number.

display_label: Emergency Contact Name
notes: NOT on original paper form. Added as required field for all student categories — critical for student welfare purposes. Collect full name of parent/guardian/relative.

display_label: Emergency Contact Number
notes: NOT on original paper form. Added as required field for all student categories — critical for student welfare purposes. Must differ from student's own active contact number ideally. Cross-field validation: emergency_contact_number must NOT equal active_contact_number (enforced server-side).

display_label: Academic Year
notes: Format: YYYY-YYYY (e.g. 2025-2026).

display_label: Semester
Year Level
notes: For returnees/transferees, pre-fill based on admission records if available.

display_label: Academic Status
notes: Not on original paper form. Added per Review suggestion 3.4. CHED may require regular vs. irregular student breakdown. Because this answer is linked to a specific submission_id in the EAV schema, status changes are automatically recorded per semester without extra fields (Fix 3). Consult registrar before marking required.

display_label: Program
notes: Both forms list the same 8 programs. Tick-box on paper — rendered as select/radio.

display_label: Surname (Apilido)
First Name
Middle Name
notes: Optional — some students have no middle name.

display_label: Religion
notes: Free-text on original form. Consider adding a select with common options + "Others" in a future revision.

display_label: Gender
notes: Exact wording from form preserved. Consider expanding options per SOGIESC guidelines in future revision.

display_label: Preferred Pronouns (Optional)
notes: Not on original paper form. Added per Review suggestion 3.1 for inclusivity. Not required by CHED but enhances student welfare data.

display_label: Marital Status
notes: Original form uses mixed case and misspellings (ANNULlED). Normalized here. Value stored as normalized string.

display_label: Present Home Address
notes: Full address as written.

display_label: Does your family own a house?
notes: Original Bisaya term "NAKIPUYU LANG" (staying with others / no own house) preserved as an option.

display_label: Barangay Name
Municipality/City
Region
notes: Consider converting to select using Philippine region list in future revision.

display_label: Estimated Household Income (per month) ₱
notes: Monthly household income in Philippine Peso. No centavos required.

display_label: Number of siblings in the family including yourself
notes: Minimum 1 (the student themselves).

display_label: I do not belong to the Indigenous Peoples (IP).
notes: If checked TRUE — hide the indigenous_peoples_group multi-select. Mutually exclusive with any IP group selection.

display_label: Please tick if you belong to any of these Indigenous Peoples
notes: Hidden when indigenous_peoples_none is true. T'BOLI spelling normalized from T'BOLI on form.

display_label: If Others — please specify IP group
notes: Shown only when Others is selected in indigenous_peoples_group.

display_label: Are you a solo parent currently studying?
notes: Footnote on form clarifies: student at the same time parent.

display_label: Are you a son/daughter of a solo parent?
notes: Footnote on form clarifies: son/daughter of a single parent.

display_label: Are you a member of Persons with Disability (PWD)?
notes: Gateway field — triggers PWD sub-section when YES.

display_label: PWD Card Status
options: I have a PWD card (submit photocopy),I do not have a PWD card and would like to obtain one,I do not have a PWD card and do not wish to obtain one
notes: REPLACES the original pwd_has_card + pwd_no_card_want_one boolean pair (Review fix 2.7 — mutually exclusive states). When value is "I have a PWD card" → require attachment_pwd_card upload. When value is "I do not have a PWD card and would like to obtain one" → trigger internal OSWD staff task/notification.

display_label: Identify your disability (please tick all that apply)
options: Apparent Physical Disability,Deaf/Hard of Hearing Disability,Visual Disability,Learning Disability,Mental/Psychosocial Disability,Speech and Language Impairment,Non-apparent Cancer,Non-apparent Rare Disease,Intellectual Disability
notes: Shown only when is_pwd is true. Original form has clarifying notes in parentheses (e.g., maglisud ug basa/sulat; bipolar, schizophrenia) — these should appear as helper text next to options, not as part of the stored value.

display_label: Participation in Sports / Literary / Dance / Music / Visual Arts in your Secondary Level
notes: New/Transfer/Returning students only. Doc Two only — Doc One (continuing) does NOT have this section. Table columns: Event Participated | Skills Competed (Specify) | Year | Award (if any). Examples on form: CRAG-Basketball-2024-Champion; RFOT-Painting-2025-Second Place. Store as repeating row sub-table.

display_label: Other skills/hobbies/talents in relation to sports, literary, dance, music, visual arts
notes: New/Transfer/Returning students only. Doc Two only. Form shows 4 numbered lines (1-4); represented as textarea. Store as free text.

display_label: What is your primary mode of residence while attending college?
options: Campus Dormitory,Off-Campus Apartment/Boarding House,Commuter Student (living with family),Commuter Student (living with relatives)
notes: Doc One uses present tense; Doc Two uses future tense. Merged to present tense as both mean the same during enrollment.

display_label: What type of cellphone do you currently have?
notes: If Basic phone selected — all subsequent internet and digital technology questions must be hidden. This is explicitly noted on both forms: 'IF YOU ANSWER BASIC PHONE THE SURVEY ENDS HERE.'

display_label: Thank you. Because you selected "Basic phone", you have completed all required questions. Please submit your form.
notes: UI-only display message — no data stored. Rendered as an info/success banner when cellphone_type = Basic phone. Replaces the paper form instruction "IF YOU ANSWER BASIC PHONE, THE SURVEY ENDS HERE. PLEASE SUBMIT." Added per Fix 5. Prefix ui_ marks it as a non-data display element.

display_label: What other gadget/s do you currently own that will aid in completing your academic requirements?
options: Smart Cellphone,Laptop/Netbook,Tablet,Both Smart Cellphone and Laptop/Netbook
notes: Shown only for Smartphone users. Doc Two wording is more precise (adds 'other') — used as master label. Doc One has same options.

display_label: What type of operating system does your cellphone have?
notes: Shown only for Smartphone users. Doc Two has explicit iOS/Android options; Doc One is vaguer. Doc Two version used as it is more accurate.

display_label: On average per week, how much do you pay to access the internet using your cellphone?
notes: Shown only for Smartphone users.

display_label: How many times do you buy a load to connect to the Internet?
options: Daily,Once a week,Two times a week,Three times a week,Only when the need arises
notes: Shown only for Smartphone users.

display_label: Does your mobile data plan have a data cap?
options: Yes, and I often run out so I need to buy another load,Yes, but I usually have enough,My mobile data are unlimited
notes: Shown only for Smartphone users. Merged wording from both docs — Doc Two adds 'megabytes, gigabytes' clarification in question; Doc One adds 'per month' — combined into plain question.

display_label: How do you avail/access internet connection in your primary residence?
options: High-speed broadband (Fiber optic, Cable),DSL (via phone line),Satellite internet (Globe, TNT, DITO, Smart),Using Piso Wifi,Internet subscription (PLDT, TETCO, Starhouse),Mobile data,No internet access in my primary residence
notes: Shown only for Smartphone users.

display_label: How do you rate the speed/reliability of your internet connection?
options: Excellent - Never have issues,Good - Minor issues occasionally,Fair - Frequent slowdowns but work usually gets done,Poor - Often too slow or disconnects,Very Poor - Unusable for academic work
notes: Shown only for Smartphone users. Doc One says 'reliability'; Doc Two says 'speed'. Merged both into label. Doc One adds clarifying examples (watching lectures, uploading assignments) — include as helper text.

display_label: When using mobile data, which mobile network are you most satisfied with for internet connectivity?
notes: Shown only for Smartphone users.

display_label: Have you ever been unable to complete or submit an assignment due to internet interruptions or lack of reliable access?
notes: Continuing students only — present in Doc One. Not in Doc Two (new/transfer/returnee). Shown only for Smartphone users.

display_label: Which campus Wi-Fi network do you primarily use?
options: NEMSU Free Wifi (DICT),BSBA Piso Wifi,I use my mobile data instead of campus Wi-Fi
notes: Continuing students only — present in Doc One. Not in Doc Two (new/transfer/returnee). Shown only for Smartphone users.

display_label: Where inside campus do you experience strong internet connectivity? (Multiple responses allowed)
options: Classrooms Agriculture,Classrooms Education (old building),BSBA Building,Library,Canteen,NEMSU Ground,Gymnasium,Everywhere is generally fine,Hostel,New Academic Building,Computer Laboratories
notes: Continuing students only — present in Doc One. Not in Doc Two (new/transfer/returnee). Shown only for Smartphone users.

display_label: Is the use of a smartphone an essential aspect of your academic life considering today's flexible learning modality?
notes: Asked of all students. Not conditional on smartphone type — even basic phone users answer this. FIXED per review 2.3: hidden for Basic phone users — paper form states "IF YOU ANSWER BASIC PHONE, THE SURVEY ENDS HERE."

display_label: If the university plans to invest in improving technology access, what would you recommend the most? (You may choose more than one)
options: Expanding campus Wi-Fi range and strength,Creating more quiet study spaces with power outlets,Offering a laptop/tablet loaner program,Providing subsidized mobile hotspots or data plans for students in need,Making the college website and learning portal more mobile-friendly
notes: Both forms have identical options. Not conditional on phone type. FIXED per review 2.3: hidden for Basic phone users — paper form states "IF YOU ANSWER BASIC PHONE, THE SURVEY ENDS HERE."

display_label: Are you willing to avail of the smartphone loan application subject to the terms and conditions initiated by the university?
notes: Both forms. Not conditional on phone type. FIXED per review 2.3: hidden for Basic phone users — paper form states "IF YOU ANSWER BASIC PHONE, THE SURVEY ENDS HERE."

display_label: What is your preferred learning modality for the coming semester, based on your enrolled program? (CHED CMO 117, s. 2026)
options: 100% on-site (face to face),75% on-site / 25% online,50% on-site / 50% online,25% on-site / 75% online
notes: Both forms. Not conditional on phone type. Reference to CHED CMO 117 s.2026 (national energy emergency flexible learning advisory). FIXED per review 2.3: hidden for Basic phone users — paper form states "IF YOU ANSWER BASIC PHONE, THE SURVEY ENDS HERE."

display_label: Upload PWD Card (photocopy)
conditional_parent: pwd_card_status=I have a PWD card (submit photocopy)
notes: Shown when is_pwd=true AND pwd_has_card=true. Original form says 'submit one photocopy' — digitized as file upload. Not on original form as a digital field — added for digital system. Conditional updated to pwd_card_status per Review fix 2.7. Conditional chain verified: is_pwd=true → pwd_card_status shown → attachment required only when pwd_card_status = "I have a PWD card (submit photocopy)" (Fix 4).

display_label: Upload Medical Certificate (if any)
notes: Optional for all categories. Not on original paper form — added for completeness per system requirements.

display_label: Upload Transfer Credentials
notes: Required for transferees only. Not on original paper form — added per system requirements. 10 MB limit — larger than other attachments (5 MB). Ensure server upload_max_filesize and post_max_size are set to at least 10 MB (Fix 6).

display_label: Upload Certificate of Good Moral Character
notes: Required for new students and transferees. Not on original paper form — added per system requirements.

display_label: Upload Letter of Intent to Return / Previous Grades
notes: Optional for returnees (students returning after leave of absence). Not on original paper form — added per Review fix 2.8. Accept letter of intent or certified true copy of previous grades.
